title: A Song of the Future.
content:
Sail fast, sail fast,
    Ark of my hopes, Ark of my dreams;
    Sweep lordly o'er the drowned Past,
    Fly glittering through the sun's strange beams;
    Sail fast, sail fast.
    Breaths of new buds from off some drying lea
    With news about the Future scent the sea:
    My brain is beating like the heart of Haste:
    I'll loose me a bird upon this Present waste;
    Go, trembling song,
    And stay not long; oh, stay not long:
    Thou'rt only a gray and sober dove,
    But thine eye is faith and thy wing is love.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Sidney Lanier, titled "A Song of the Future.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
